front: What is cryo-electron microscopy (cryo-EM) and how does it determine protein structures?
back: Cryo-EM flash-freezes proteins in vitreous ice, collecting 2D projection images. Single-particle analysis classifies and averages thousands of images to reconstruct 3D structures. Resolution revolution: direct electron detectors and software (RELION, cryoSPARC) enable near-atomic resolution.
